I hear you got cut!  Use a clean cloth, towel, or gauze and press hard directly on the wound. Do not peek to check if it stopped; keep pressing for at least 5–10 minutes without letting go. Elevate your hand above your heart if possible.

I hear you got cut!Once the bleeding slows down, take a quick look. If the cut is deep (you can see yellow fat or bone), longer than half an inch, or if the blood is spurting out, you need emergency medical care immediately for stitches. Do not wait. If it's a shallow cut, clean it gently with water once bleeding stops.

I hear you got cut!Once the bleeding stops, rinse gently with clean running water. Do not use hydrogen peroxide or alcohol inside the wound as it damages tissue. Apply an antibiotic ointment if you have one, and cover it with a sterile bandage. Keep it dry.

I hear you blowing on your hands and rubbing them together — it sounds freezing out there! That's a great way to get some quick warmth back into your fingers. Make sure to keep moving to keep your blood flowing.

That sounds chilly. While rubbing helps, remember that warming your core is key. If you can, tuck your hands into your armpits or pockets, or hug your body to trap heat. Don't let your hands stay exposed for too long. Find a warm spot as soon as you can.

Please be careful not to stay out in the cold too long. Check your fingers regularly — if they turn white, numb, or hard, get indoors immediately to prevent frostbite. Avoid rubbing too hard if the skin feels numb, as it can cause damage. Warm up gently with lukewarm water once inside.

Hot oil burn detected! STOP what you are doing immediately. Run cool(not cold) for at least 15–20 minutes. Do not stop early; this is crucial to stop the heat from damaging deeper skin layers. Keep the water running gently.

Oil burn detected!Do NOT apply ice, butter, toothpaste, soy sauce, or any ointment right now. These can trap the heat or damage the tissue further. Do not rub the area or try to wipe the oil off aggressively; just let the water wash it away naturally. Only use cool running water.

Hot oil burn detected!Run cool water right now!It should continue at least 15-20 minutes.After cooling, check the area. If the skin turns white, charred, or if large blisters form, seek medical attention immediately.

I hear you blowing on it — that helps a little, but it's not enough for a hot burn. The heat is still trapped deep in your skin. Please stop blowing and go immediately to a sink. Run cool (not cold) over the area for at least 15–20 minutes. Water removes heat much faster than air.

Blowing helps slightly, but you need stronger cooling. If you can't get to running water right now, apply a clean, cool wet cloth to the burn and replace it as soon as it warms up. Do not use ice directly.
Sounds like something delicious but piping hot! Take your time blowing on it; there's no rush. It's always better to let it cool down a bit than to burn your tongue. Enjoy your meal!
